business.headline: Oil India reports second gas discovery in Andaman offshore blockOil India has reported natural gas in the Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman offshore basin. The find adds to evidence of hydrocarbon potential in the block as appraisal work continues.

India-US trade deal's first tranche likely by mid-July, says Piyush GoyalSpeaking about the discussions, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said the meetings held between June 2 and June 4 were very encouraging.

India's economy grows 7.7% in FY26 despite global turmoil, war concernsProvisional government estimates show economic growth accelerated from the previous year, with robust expansion in the January-March quarter helping lift full-year GDP growth to 7.7%.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as RBI holds rates; rupee logs biggest gain since AprilMarkets ended lower after the RBI kept the repo rate at 5.25%, while the rupee posted its biggest one-day gain since April. Investors are now weighing inflation, crude prices, foreign outflows and West Asia tensions for clues on market direction.

Gold, FDs or real estate? I asked experts where to invest during rupee weaknessWhen the rupee comes under pressure, many investors start looking for a safe haven. But which asset actually delivers—gold, FDs or real estate? I spoke to experts to find out.

Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

The hidden inflation nobody is talking about: Transport inflationAs transportation costs increase across the supply chain, businesses eventually pass those higher expenses on to consumers. The result is 'invisible inflation' – a force that pushes up the cost of almost everything but is rarely talked about on its own.

RBI MPC meeting: 7 key takeaways on repo rate, inflation, growth and moreThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its latest monetary policy decisions, keeping the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and maintaining a neutral stance. Here are seven key takeaways from the RBI's June MPC meeting.

Rajesh Exports shares hit 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight day after Sebi orderRajesh Exports shares hit the 5% lower circuit for a second straight session after Sebi's interim order. The sell-off reflects investor caution as the regulator examines alleged revenue inflation and orders a fresh forensic audit.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for your home loan and EMIFor millions of home loan borrowers, the RBI's repo rate decision is closely watched because it can influence borrowing costs. With the central bank keeping the repo rate steady at 5.25%, should you expect any change in your monthly EMI?

RBI rolls out multiple measures to attract more dollars amid rupee weaknessThe RBI has unveiled a package of bond, swap and deposit measures to draw more dollars into India while keeping the repo rate unchanged. The steps are aimed at easing foreign currency liquidity strains as oil prices rise and investor outflows weigh on the rupee.

Higher crude prices forcing a rethink on inflation and growth outlook, says RBIThe RBI kept the repo rate unchanged as Sanjay Malhotra warned that the West Asia conflict is lifting oil prices and disrupting supplies. The central bank has cut its growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook as cost pressures begin to build.

Centre exempts foreign investors from capital gains tax on govt bondsThe tax break is aimed at boosting foreign investment in government bonds, improving post-tax returns for overseas investors and helping attract more stable capital flows into India.

RBI keeps repo rate steady at 5.25%, reduces GDP growth projection for FY27All six members of the MPC voted unanimously to maintain the policy repo rate at 5.25%. The central bank also retained its "neutral" policy stance, signalling that future decisions will depend on incoming economic data and evolving global developments.

PwC review flags accounting irregularities in IndusInd Bank treasury operationsA confidential PwC review reportedly found that manual treasury entries at IndusInd Bank offset trading losses and created a Rs 2,201.76 crore receivable pool. The findings have raised fresh questions over hedging links, accounting treatment and controls around manual adjustments.

Markets open higher as investors await RBI rate decision; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 191.96 points, or 0.26%, to 74,551.97 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 39 points, or 0.17%, to 23,455.55.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall or rise on RBI MPC decision?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,565.00 at 8:17 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 could open above its previous close of 23,416.55.

Gujarat wire-free cities plan: Urban power lines to go underground by 2030Gujarat has approved a statewide mission to move urban power lines underground by 2030. The phased project aims to improve power reliability and safety while making cities less cluttered.

RBI ends FEMA probe against Myntra. Here's what happenedAccording to the Enforcement Directorate (ED), the RBI passed the order on April 20, 2026 under Section 15 of FEMA after receiving a "No Objection" from the agency. As a result, the investigation against the company has been terminated.

LIC owns 10% of Rajesh Exports, shares fall 1% at closing bell amid Sebi allegationsShareholding data for the March 2026 quarter shows that Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC), the country's largest insurer and institutional investor, holds a 10.80% stake in Rajesh Exports.

entertainment.headline: Farah Khan quips about Vashu Bhagnani amid his legal row with David Dhawan Farah Khan joked about Vashu Bhagnani while speaking to Varun and David Dhawan in her vlog. The exchange drew notice because Vashu and David are locked in a legal dispute over Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai.

Office Romance review: Jennifer Lopez can do many things. Saving the film isn't oneRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Office Romance, starring Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein, released on Netflix with boardroom chaos and a workplace love story. Slick and watchable, the movie, however, never quite finds the charm or chemistry that makes a romcom linger.

Balan The Boy trailer: Mystery and murder haunt a mother's fight to protect her son The trailer of Balan: The Boy offers a chilling glimpse into a mother-son duo caught in a web of secrets, danger and survival. Directed by Chidambaram and written by Jithu Madhavan, the psychological thriller is set for a June 19 release.

Buffy and Ted Lasso actor Anthony Head dies at 72 Anthony Head has died at 72 after complications from pneumonia, his daughters said. The British actor was remembered for a decades-long screen career spanning Buffy the Vampire Slayer, Ted Lasso and other series.

Ram Charan's Japanese fans fly to Hyderabad, plan to watch Peddi 10 times. Video Two die-hard Ram Charan fans from Japan travelled to Hyderabad to watch Peddi on its opening day. The duo took a five-day break from work and plans to watch the sports drama 10 times with local fans.

Cocktail 2 BTS: Rashmika Mandanna teases director Homi in fun online exchange Rashmika Mandanna shared a playful behind-the-scenes moment with director Homi Adajania on Instagram, teasing him over a photo from the sets of Cocktail 2. The highly anticipated spiritual sequel, which also stars Shahid Kapoor and Kriti Sanon, is set to hit theaters on June 19, 2026.

Peddi director shocked by criticism over Janhvi Kapoor's hypersexualisation Director Buchi Babu Sana has said he did not expect the backlash over Peddi's scenes featuring Janhvi Kapoor's character. His remarks acknowledge wider criticism that the film's romance track normalised harassment and non-consent.

The Marked Woman review: A stylish Spanish noir with secrets in plain sightRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Netflix's The Marked Woman review: Spanish stars Ana Rujas, Candela Pena and Pol Lopez elevate this stylish neo-noir thriller. Moody, atmospheric and gripping, even when its twists feel familiar.

Nagarjuna claps board for Dhootha 2 as Naga Chaitanya turns producer Season 2 of Dhootha was launched with a pooja ceremony where Nagarjuna clapped the board for Naga Chaitanya and the cast. The new season marks Chaitanya's debut as a producer.

Ice Age: Boiling Point teaser reunites Manny, Sid and Diego; release date announced The first teaser for Ice Age: Boiling Point reunites Manny, Sid, Diego and the rest of the herd for another adventure packed with chaos and comedy. The makers have also announced the release date for the sixth instalment of the beloved animated franchise.

Sharon Stone recalls how husband abandoned her during a breast cancer scare Sharon Stone said her ex-husband Phil Bronstein walked out after doctors urged a bilateral mastectomy during her breast cancer scare. She said his reaction showed the marriage was over and underscored her insistence on deciding her own treatment.

Shilpa Shinde's false sexual harassment claims power patriarchy. Women lose the most Shilpa Shinde has said she filed a false sexual harassment complaint against producer Sanjay Kohli during their dispute. Doesn't it raise questions about accountability, reputational harm and the effect on women reporting workplace harassment?

Mollywood Times review: Naslen's film is a love-hate letter to Malayalam CinemaRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Mollywood Times movie review: Director Abhinav Sunder Nayak's Mollywood Times follows Vineeth Madhavan from cinephile boyhood to the bruising realities of filmmaking. The film turns that journey into a reflection on obsession, compromise and the dream of reaching Friday.

The Odyssey: India gets early IMAX ticket access; here's when bookings begin Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is opening its ticket booking window, with India among the first markets in the world to go live. Here's when fans can secure their seats for the film's IMAX run.

Akshay Kumar pulls horse prank on Rajpal Yadav in Welcome To The Jungle BTS clip Akshay Kumar shared a behind-the-scenes clip of a horse startling Rajpal Yadav on the sets of Welcome To The Jungle. The playful moment ended in laughter, with the actor teasing the film's wild comic energy ahead of its June 26, 2026 release.

When Janhvi spoke about being hypersexualised; clip resurfaces amid Peddi row As Peddi faces criticism for allegedly objectifying Janhvi Kapoor's character, the actor's earlier remarks on consent and over-sexualisation in films have resurfaced online.

Janhvi Kapoor's hypersexualisation in Peddi sparks outrage, internet is appalled Peddi has drawn criticism over the way Janhvi Kapoor's character Achiyyamma is written and filmed. The backlash has reignited debate over consent, sexualisation and agency in mainstream cinema.

Jana Nayagan makers to refund distributors as CM Vijay's film remains in limbo The producers of actor-politician Vijay's Jana Nayagan have plans to refund distributor advances as the film awaits clearance for an independent release. The move follows months of certification delays, legal setbacks and mounting losses across the chain.

Taylor Swift's net worth: The road to a $2 billion empire without being The Man Taylor Swift has reportedly crossed the $2 billion net worth mark, becoming the richest female musician in history. Here's how touring, music ownership and record-breaking releases fuelled her financial rise.

general news.headline: India has never followed diktat from abroad: Putin's message to Trump at SPIEFPutin, speaking at the SPIEF 2026, underlined that sovereignty and sovereign decision-making can never be contested, as he sent a stern message to Trump on attempts to pressure India to scale back its cooperation with Russia.

Annamalai chooses a brand new icon: APJ Abdul KalamAfter resigning from the BJP, K Annamalai announced that he would be launching a political movement that would gradually transition to a political party. Annamalai said that the movement would function under the APJ Abdul Kalam Centre for Ethics and Politics. Here's why invoking Kalam in Tamil Nadu politics is a masterstroke.

Putin praises India's talent, says its coders are renowned across the worldSpeaking at the plenary session of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF), moderated by India Today's Geeta Mohan, Russian President Vladimir Putin said India and Russia share strong, trust-based and "brotherly" relations. He also praised India's human capital, noting that the country is globally recognised for its skilled workforce, especially in the technology sector.
The OG Cockroach Janta PartyThe real Cockroach Janata Party has been in power since 1947. It has no manifesto, holds no rallies, and contests no elections. It just continues. It survives everything.

Janhvi Kapoor's hypersexualisation in Peddi sparks outrage, internet is appalledPeddi has drawn criticism over the way Janhvi Kapoor's character Achiyyamma is written and filmed. The backlash has reignited debate over consent, sexualisation and agency in mainstream cinema.

Sanctions threats against India will boomerang under PM Modi, says PutinResponding to questions from India Today's Geeta Mohan at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF), Putin said India has consistently pursued policies guided by its national interests and would continue to do so regardless of external pressure over its ties with Russia.

Alexander Zverev keeps Grand Slam dream alive, storms into French Open finalFrench Open 2026: World No.3 Alexander Zverev beat Jakub Mensik 7-5, 6-2, 3-6, 6-3 on Friday and stormed into the final, where he will face the winner Flavio Cobolli, who advanced after Matteo Arnaldi pulled out of his semifinal due to a virus.

Will Putin be up for meeting Zelenskyy? Here's what he told India Today at SPIEFResponding to a question from India Today's Geeta Mohan at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF), Putin said he was not opposed to meeting Zelenskyy in principle but insisted that any summit must produce meaningful results rather than serve as a symbolic diplomatic exercise.

One CM (Vijay) can't change everything: Annamalai's only political message on TNIn his first address after quitting the BJP, K Annamalai announced his plans for a political movement and a party. In the 23-minute-long speech, Annamalai had only one message about Tamil Nadu's current political regime. Without naming C Joseph Vijay, Annamalai said just one chief minister cannot bring all the change needed in the state.

Putin decodes why Brics nations are moving beyond the dollarAt the SPIEF plenary session moderated by India Today's Geeta Mohan, Vladimir Putin argued that growing distrust of Western sanctions, financial controls and asset freezes is pushing Brics nations and other countries away from the US dollar and euro.

US attacks Iranian radar sites after Tehran launches drones towards HormuzFollowing the interceptions, US forces targeted Iranian radar installations in Goruk and on Qeshm Island. CENTCOM described the operation as a defensive measure aimed at preventing further attacks and protecting freedom of navigation in the region.

Putin slams Zelenskyy's 'rude' letter to him over Ukraine talks, praises TrumpRussian President Vladimir Putin criticised Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's open appeal for face-to-face talks, saying the letter contained "rude" remarks and reflected Kyiv's double standards on peace negotiations. He also commended US President Donald Trump for his efforts to broker an end to the conflict.

Peddi review: Ram Charan and the emotions hit sixes, the writing gets run outRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026Ram Charan's Peddi follows the journey of a daily-wage labourer from a nameless village who takes on a larger fight for a railway station, dignity, and identity. Despite noticeable logic gaps, underdeveloped romance, and uneven editing, the film succeeds where it matters most, emotionally.

Brics has overtaken G7: Putin shows mirror to Trump at SPIEF 2026Russian President Putin's firm endorsement of the Brics comes at a time when US President Donald Trump has railed against the grouping and called it a 'threat' to the US dollar.

Monsoon on the move: Heavy rain likely in North and South India SaturdayThe southwest monsoon continues its steady advance across India, bringing heavy rains to the south. Northern regions brace for gusty winds and thunderstorms this Saturday, June 6.

E85 fuel launched in Delhi at Rs 82.12/litre, first pump opens at Pusa RoadIndia has taken a major step in its ethanol-blending journey with the launch of E85 fuel in Delhi. Priced lower than E20 petrol, the new fuel promises reduced oil dependence and lower running costs.

How Sunil Dutt saved the Bandra slum suburbia, built a Congress vote bankAs Mumbai demolishes slums from Garib Nagar to Goregaon, an old debate has returned. Did Sunil Dutt protect the poor, or help institutionalise Mumbai's slum politics? Here's a look at how Dutt's Partition trauma, Congress's patronage and suburban migration reshaped Bandra, and Mumbai's politics itself.

CBSE extends Class 12 verification, re-evaluation application deadline till June 7CBSE has extended the deadline for Class 12 verification and re-evaluation applications by one day, from June 6 (midnight) to June 7 (midnight), to give students additional time to complete the process.

Gill's India seek red-ball rhythm vs Afghanistan before crucial WTC stretchIndia vs Afghanistan, one-off Test preview: Shubman Gill and his men will be looking to switch to red-ball mode before a tough stretch of the World Test Championship cycle. Visitors Afghanistan will be keen on putting up a good show in New Chandigarh, which is hosting a Test match for the first time.

Against hegemony, double standard: China jabs US at SPIEF, calls for greater UN roleChinese Vice President Han Zheng used the SPIEF 2026 plenary session to call for a stronger role for the United Nations and greater international cooperation, while warning against hegemony and double standards in global affairs.

Were drumbeats for Rabi Lamichhane in India meant for PM Balen's ears in Nepal?Handcrafted ByOur Editors The grand welcome accorded to Rabi Lamichhane, Chairman of Nepal's ruling Rashtriya Swatantra Party, in India has made everyone sit up and take note. The question being asked is it a signal for PM Balendra 'Balen' Shah, whose remarks have triggered controversy in both Nepal and India?

R Praggnanandhaa scripts history, becomes first Indian to win Norway Chess titleR Praggnanandhaa scripted history by becoming the first Indian to win the Norway Chess title, capping a stunning comeback with a final-round victory over Vincent Keymer in Oslo.

What will happen to E20 vehicles when ethanol blending goes beyond 20%?As India prepares for the next phase of ethanol blending, questions are emerging over the future of millions of E20-compatible vehicles and whether they can adapt to higher ethanol concentrations. Let's find out.

Bandar review: Tough questions and easy answers in Bobby Deol's unsettling filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026Bandar movie review: Anurag Kashyap's Bandar follows fading star Samar Mehra (Bobby Deol) as a rape accusation pushes him into police custody and jail. The film dwells on systemic humiliation and prison brutality, even as its moral perspective narrows.

sports.headline: England seize control as New Zealand slump to 36 for 3 at Lord's ENG vs NZ: England moved to the brink of victory in the first Test at Lord's after reducing New Zealand to 36 for 3 in a chase of 254. Earlier, Ollie Robinson claimed a five-wicket haul on his Test return, while Nathan Smith's six-for kept New Zealand in the contest.

R Praggnanandhaa scripts history, becomes first Indian to win Norway Chess title R Praggnanandhaa scripted history by becoming the first Indian to win the Norway Chess title, capping a stunning comeback with a final-round victory over Vincent Keymer in Oslo.

India suffer 1-3 defeat to Tajikistan in international friendly India suffered a 1-3 defeat to Tajikistan in the first of two international friendlies at the TALCO Arena in Tursunzoda on Friday. Sheriddin Boboev, Mekhrubon Karimov and Ehsoni Panshanbe scored for the hosts, while Farukh Choudhary netted a late consolation goal for India in the 88th minute.

Iran players granted visas for US World Cup, set for June 15 opener in LA Iran's national football team has officially secured U.S. visas just ten days before their highly anticipated World Cup opener in Los Angeles. The squad will touch down in Tijuana this Sunday to finalize preparations amid intense political tensions.

Sunil Gavaskar pauses speech to answer wife's call, delights Mumbai audience Sunil Gavaskar paused his address in Mumbai to answer a call from his wife, Marshneil. The spontaneous exchange drew laughter at the venue and admiration across social media.

Virus forces Arnaldi out, setting up Zverev vs Cobolli French Open final French Open: Matteo Arnaldi withdrew from his semi-final against Flavio Cobolli after being struck by a virus, ending his impressive run in Paris. The late withdrawal sent Cobolli into his maiden Grand Slam final, where he will face Alexander Zverev on Sunday.

Alexander Zverev takes cheeky dig at father after reaching French Open final After booking his place in the French Open final with a hard-fought victory over Jakub Mensik on Friday, Alexander Zverev took a cheeky dig at his father and coach during his post-match interview.

India vs Afghanistan, One-off Test livestreaming: Where to watch Mullanpur Test India will face Afghanistan in a one-off Test in Mullanpur from June 6. The match gives India a key red-ball tune-up ahead of a demanding schedule.

Alexander Zverev keeps Grand Slam dream alive, storms into French Open final French Open 2026: World No.3 Alexander Zverev beat Jakub Mensik 7-5, 6-2, 3-6, 6-3 on Friday and stormed into the final, where he will face the winner Flavio Cobolli, who advanced after Matteo Arnaldi pulled out of his semifinal due to a virus.

Shubman Gill embraces home Test challenge after IPL final setback Shubman Gill has shifted his focus from the IPL to leading India against Afghanistan in New Chandigarh. The one-off Test offers India a chance to assess fresh talent and build momentum before a demanding run.

FIFA cancels dozens of World Cup tickets issued for free by mistake FIFA has cancelled World Cup tickets issued to around 60 fans after a website glitch allowed them to secure seats without making any payment, adding another chapter to the controversy surrounding the tournament's ticketing system.

Gill's India seek red-ball rhythm vs Afghanistan before crucial WTC stretchIndia vs Afghanistan, one-off Test preview: Shubman Gill and his men will be looking to switch to red-ball mode before a tough stretch of the World Test Championship cycle. Visitors Afghanistan will be keen on putting up a good show in New Chandigarh, which is hosting a Test match for the first time.

Rohit doesn't need to prove himself, India need him in World Cup 2027: Dinesh LadDinesh Lad, the childhood coach who played a key role in shaping Rohit Sharma's career, has urged the former India captain to block out criticism over his fitness and form, saying the veteran opener should focus on enjoying his cricket as he gears up for the ODI World Cup next year.

Andy Robertson joins Tottenham on free after Liverpool exitTottenham Hotspur have confirmed Andy Robertson on a free transfer after his Liverpool exit. The move gives Roberto De Zerbi an experienced leader as Spurs begin a defensive rebuild.

James Rodríguez under fire over Antonella Petro snubA video from Colombia's send-off ceremony appears to show James Rodríguez ignoring Antonella Petro as she approached him. The clip triggered a political storm, with online criticism and rivals debating whether the moment was deliberate.

Semifinal Highlights: Zverev set to face Cobolli in final after Arnaldi withdrawsFrench Open Men's singles semifinal LIVE: World No. 3 Alexander Zverev will face Flavio Cobolli in the men's singles final. While Zeverv stormed into the final after beating Jakub Mensik in four sets in the first semi-final of the day, the next one saw an abrupt end as Arnaldi withdrew from his clash against Cobolli due to apparent illness or food poisoning.

Explained: Why KL Rahul went unsold in the Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 auctionKL Rahul went unsold in the Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 2026 auction after franchises were informed that he was unavailable for the tournament. With the India star expected to be on national duty during the competition, teams chose to prioritise players who would be available throughout the season rather than spend on a marquee name unlikely to feature.

ICC's pink-ball trial for day Test matches gets India coach Gambhir's approvalIndia coach Gautam Gambhir welcomed the ICC's decision to allow the use of pink balls during day Tests, saying the move would help teams force results in matches disrupted by bad weather and poor light.

Khare's century drives Raipur Rhinos to commanding CCPL opener winAmandeep Khare struck 102 as Raipur Rhinos beat Rajnandgaon Panthers by 90 runs in the CCPL opener. The knock underlined his standing in Chhattisgarh cricket as Raipur began Season 3 strongly.

Half of the car was gone: Michael Clarke recalls serious crash after IPL finalFormer Australian captain Michael Clarke escaped with minor injuries after his car suffered a severe crash on the way to the airport following the IPL 2026 final. While Clarke survived with bruises, his driver sustained suspected fractures.

Onus on ICC: Afghanistan coach seeks more Tests to grow red-ball cricket in the countryAfghanistan head coach Richard Pybus has urged the ICC to provide emerging Test nations with a proper fixture schedule, saying consistent red-ball cricket is essential for development. He also stressed that one-off matches are not enough and called for longer bilateral Test series against top teams.

India very optimistic about qualifying for Test Championship final: Gautam GambhirGautam Gambhir said India remain confident of reaching the World Test Championship final despite a difficult start to the cycle. He said the young side's belief, continuity and upcoming Tests will shape the qualification push.

FIFA World Cup 2026 big misses: Stars missing out on tournament due to injuryAs the build-up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 continues, these players will not be a part of the tournament due to injury. The likes of Rodrygo and Hugo Ekitike will be big misses for their sides as they chase the title.

Is Rishabh Pant's over-aggressive Test approach a problem? Coach Gambhir reactsIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir backed Rishabh Pant's aggressive style ahead of the Afghanistan Test but stressed that international cricket requires players to respect match situations. Pant remains under scrutiny following a series of questionable dismissals and his recent removal as India's Test vice-captain.

Sai Sudharsan or Devdutt Padikkal? Gambhir reveals India's No.3 for AFG TestGautam Gambhir has indicated that Sai Sudharsan is ahead of Devdutt Padikkal for India's No. 3 spot in the Afghanistan Test. Gambhir backed Sudharsan, saying that he hasn't got a fair shot while batting at the spot.

5000 to 5 million: Least-known World Cup star meets influencer who made him famousArgentine influencer Valen Scarsini sought out Tim Payne after his online campaign turned the New Zealand defender into a viral World Cup figure. The episode shows how quickly digital culture can recast a fringe player as a tournament talking point.

Skyfall: Did Suryakumar Yadav deserve to be sacked without a final act?Three months after lifting the T20 World Cup in Ahmedabad, Suryakumar Yadav has been sacked as India's captain. A late bloomer's extraordinary rise is over. But did he deserve better than this?

Football fever grips Kerala, fans carry giant Brazil banner ahead of FIFA World CupKerala has welcomed the World Cup with giant banners, flares and rival colours on its streets. In Malappuram, the celebrations have turned anticipation into memory, as fans savour what they call a last dance.

Vinesh-WFI battle continues, wrestlers conduct at national trials under lensThe Wrestling Federation of India has indirectly hinted that Vinesh Phogat's conduct at the national trials will come under the scanner after a chaotic tournament.

Group D Preview: Can hosts USA hold off Turkiye's golden generation?The United States head into a volatile Group D as co-hosts, with Turkiye, Paraguay and Australia all in contention. A wide-open race, led by a marquee USA-Turkiye clash, could leave qualification undecided until the final matchday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
